channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why scientists are nervous about fungi
item.description: They can pose a threat to human health — yeast infections are but one example. Scientists say not enough attention is paid to their ability to develop resistance to medications that treat them.
item.content:encoded:
They can pose a threat to human health — yeast infections are but one example. Scientists say not enough attention is paid to their ability to develop resistance to medications that treat them.

(Image credit: Shawn Lockhart)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Leaders urge for restraint as 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Hezbollah, the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group, acknowledged the ceasefire, but did not say whether it would abide by it and urged people displaced by war in Lebanon to refrain from heading home.
item.content:encoded:
Hezbollah, the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group, acknowledged the ceasefire, but did not say whether it would abide by it and urged people displaced by war in Lebanon to refrain from heading home.

(Image credit: IBRAHIM AMRO)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
item.description: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb
item.content:encoded:
A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

(Image credit: Adri Salido)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Who says they have no fear of the Trump administration? The quiz knows
item.description: Also: If you know what Eric Swalwell looks like, you'll get at least one question correct.
item.content:encoded: Also: If you know what Eric Swalwell looks like, you'll get at least one question correct.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Do less, ask for more: How to make life easier as a working parent
item.description: Going back to work after having a baby can be overwhelming. You're juggling all the emotions of being a new parent while getting up to speed at your job. Tips to help you make a smooth transition.
item.content:encoded: Going back to work after having a baby can be overwhelming. You're juggling all the emotions of being a new parent while getting up to speed at your job. Tips to help you make a smooth transition.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: The Labor Department wants to teach you to use AI more. Here's what we found
item.description: The short course provides solid basics for using AI. But it also misidentifies AI products, links out to bad advice and raises ethical concerns about the products it promotes
item.content:encoded:
The short course provides solid basics for using AI. But it also misidentifies AI products, links out to bad advice and raises ethical concerns about the products it promotes

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Families left reeling after hospitals in blue states drop transgender care for youth
item.description: Massachusetts passed laws and joined lawsuits to protect access to gender-affirming care for minors. But faced with the Trump administration's threats, some hospitals voluntarily stopped care.
item.content:encoded:
Massachusetts passed laws and joined lawsuits to protect access to gender-affirming care for minors. But faced with the Trump administration's threats, some hospitals voluntarily stopped care.

(Image credit: Karen Brown)
item.dc:creator: Karen Brown

item.title: Logjam of U.S. immigration applications puts millions at greater risk of deportation
item.description: An NPR analysis shows how immigrants' attempts to live or work legally in the U.S. are caught in a bureaucratic morass.
item.content:encoded:
An NPR analysis shows how immigrants' attempts to live or work legally in the U.S. are caught in a bureaucratic morass.

(Image credit: Damian Dovarganes)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Human rights groups raise alarm over fate of Salvadorans deported from U.S.
item.description: Migrants deported from the U.S. routinely disappear into El Salvador's prisons the moment they land or in the weeks that follow. Many remain incommunicado from family and lawyers for years.
item.content:encoded:
Migrants deported from the U.S. routinely disappear into El Salvador's prisons the moment they land or in the weeks that follow. Many remain incommunicado from family and lawyers for years.

(Image credit: Illustration by Jackie Lay/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, U.S. military officials say the blockade of Iranian ports and ceasefire is holding, Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, U.S. military officials say the blockade of Iranian ports and ceasefire is holding, Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
